subject: feat(burger-menu): add external / iconClass / highlightColor item fields
body:
Close the API gap vs the BURGER_MENU pattern's standard MenuItem interface.
All three fields were flagged in the post-picker audit as pattern features
we hadn't shipped.

- `external?: boolean` — marks an item as pointing outside the app. Renders
  a trailing ↗ icon after the label. ariaLabel gets "(opens in new tab)"
  appended so screen readers announce the behavior even when the label
  doesn't mention it. Use case (future): "User Guide", "Docs" items.
- `iconClass?: string` — per-icon color-class override. Defaults to
  `currentColor` via the item's state-derived text color. Use case
  (future): a warning-colored icon on an otherwise-neutral item.
- `highlightColor?: string` — alternative accent text-color class when
  `highlight: true`. Defaults to `text-primary`. Use case (future): a
  success-green "new!" highlight alongside the existing primary-tinted
  "Install app" item.

No current consumer uses any of these — they're shipped for pattern
conformance so a future reader comparing our impl against BURGER_MENU.md
sees the full MenuAction surface. Each field defaults to the existing
behavior when omitted, so every existing menu-item spec in the app
continues to work unchanged.

Tags: feature, burger-menu, api, pattern-compliance
Complexity: 1
Urgency: 1
Impact: internal
Risk: low

title: add external / iconClass / highlightColor item fields
